commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Let not my lord, I pray you, regard this man of Belial, even Nabal: for as his name is, so is he; Nabal is his name, and folly is with him: but I your handmaid saw not the young men of my lord, whom you did send. The king, is not in Hebrew, Septuagint David's title was not yet publicly acknowledged. (Calmet) But Abigail plainly alludes to it, ver. (Haydock) Name. Nabal, in Hebrew, signifies a fool. (Calmet) Thus she extenuates his fault, by attributing it to a deficiency in understanding.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: His name: Nabal, in Hebrew, signifies a fool.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
